name: Chistabín Aragonese (Gistaín/Chistau valley)

ancestors.notes: Chistabín is a Central/Eastern High-Aragonese valley variety inheriting the Aragonese grapheme→IPA system; it declares the geminate and final-r deltas and documents lexical/morphological distinctiveness in notes.
Hispanic Vulgar Latin, inherited via Aragonese (an).
Gascon adstrate on the eastern Sobrarbe fringe, inherited via Aragonese; first-person plural verb ending -m parallels Ribagorçan.

notes: Chistabín — the Aragonese of the Gistaín/Chistau valley (Valle de Chistau, Sobrarbe, Huesca), a Central/Eastern High-Aragonese variety, comparatively well preserved (references: Blas Gabarda & Romanos Hernando 2008 'Diccionario aragonés chistabín'; Mott 1989). PHONOLOGICALLY-EXPRESSED DELTAS modelled here: (1) PRESERVED GEMINATES -LL-/-NN- as long consonants in inherited words, written ⟨l·l⟩/⟨n·n⟩: bel·lota [beˈlːota], pen·na [ˈpenːa], cabán·na [kaˈbanːa] (read at retazosgistain.wordpress.com 'Habla del Valle de Gistau', a secondary summary of Blas Gabarda & Romanos / Mott). Modelled with ⟨l·l⟩→[lː], ⟨n·n⟩→[nː]. (2) word-final ⟨r⟩ DELETED (the infinitive comère surfaces as comé), modelled as ⟨r⟩ word_final → [∅], base [r]/[ɾ] elsewhere preserved. The prepalatal fricative ⟨x⟩/⟨ix⟩ = [ʃ] with an i-glide/prosthesis (ixufrina [iʃuˈfɾina], buixo [ˈbwiʃo]) is already yielded by the base an ⟨ix⟩ = [ʃ ...] and the prosthetic ⟨i⟩ that the spelling carries, so no delta is needed. Chistabín keeps the Western/Central profile from an (F- preserved, /θ/ distinción, ⟨ch⟩=[tʃ]). FEATURES NOT PHONEME-MAPPABLE (documented, not modelled — phonemic spelling, lexical/morphological): -CT- → -t- (feto); voicing of intervocalic Latin voiceless stops (caixigo, cadiera, radigón); article el/la/es/las plus contracted a/as; first-person-plural verb ending -m (vs general -mos); participles -au/-iu; past-participle agreement (las vacas las han comidas). Diphthongisation Ĕ/Ŏ → ie/ue inherited (Várvaro, AFA XLVI-XLVII, p. 251: Gistaín shows ué). PROVENANCE HONESTY: valley phonetics read from a secondary online summary of Blas Gabarda & Romanos (2008) and Mott (1989); the primary monographs were not directly obtainable. Várvaro (AFA) read directly.

sources.author: Blas Gabarda, F. & Romanos Hernando, F.
sources.title: Diccionario aragonés chistabín (Bal de Chistau)
sources.publisher: Gara d'Edizions / Institución Fernando el Católico
sources.notes: Reference grammar/dictionary of Chistabín. Cited via a secondary online summary (retazosgistain.wordpress.com); primary not directly obtainable in this work.

sources.author: Mott, B.
sources.title: El habla de Gistaín
sources.notes: Source of the intervocalic-voicing and geminate observations. Cited via secondary summary; primary not directly obtainable.

sources.author: Várvaro, A.
sources.title: De la escritura al habla: la diptongación de O breve tónica en el Alto Aragón
sources.notes: Read directly. Gistaín shows the ué diphthong (p. 251).

location.notes: the Gistaín/Chistau valley (Sobrarbe, Huesca) — the valley the variety is named for and confined to. [Wikidata Q3102588 P625]
